journal_entry.description: Employee meal benefit for overtime workers paid via corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: The meal provided to employees working overtime is an employee benefit expense. Under IFRS, employee benefits are recognized as an expense when the entity consumes the economic benefit arising from service provided by an employee. The corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). No tax is applicable as meals provided directly to employees as a benefit are not subject to HST/GST input tax credits in the same way as general business meals.

output.reason: The credit side records the corporate credit card obligation. When Mondelez uses the corporate credit card to pay for the meal, the company incurs a liability to the credit card company. This is a liability increase (credit card payable).

credit_classifier.reason: The credit side records the corporate credit card obligation. When Mondelez uses the corporate credit card to pay for the meal, the company incurs a liability to the credit card company. This is a liability increase (credit card payable).

output.reason: The meal provided to employees working overtime is an employee benefit expense. This represents compensation/benefit provided to employees, which is an operating expense. Debit: 1 expense increase (employee benefits/meals expense).
